pr.title: Add translation unit support for decomp.py
pr.author: thefoxcam

summary: Updated tools/decomp.py so its single positional m2c input can be either a function name or a translation-unit path such as melee/it/items/itheiho. The script now attempts to resolve the input as a function via find_obj/has_function; if no object containing that function is found, it treats the input as a translation unit, builds the corresponding ASM path, and invokes m2c without --function. --write remains function-only and now errors for translation-unit input.
what_changed: Changed the argparse description and positional argument from a function-only input to m2c_input, documented as accepting either a function name like it_8026B9A8 or a translation unit path like melee/it/items/itheiho.
Kept one positional input instead of requiring separate --function or --translation-unit flags in the final diff shown.
Added input classification logic: call find_obj(OBJ_ROOT, m2c_input); if it finds an object containing that function, use the matching .s file and pass --function m2c_input to m2c.
Added translation-unit fallback: if find_obj returns None, treat m2c_input as a path under ASM_ROOT with .s suffix and run m2c without a --function argument.
Changed --write help text to clarify it is function-input only.
Added an explicit error path for --write with translation-unit input: print that it is currently unimplemented and exit.
Guarded write-back with is_function so translation-unit decomp output is not inserted into a source file.
Changed the not-found error from function-oriented wording to an ASM-path error: 'Asm file does not exist at path {asm_file}'.
smart_moves: Resolved the CLI ambiguity by reusing existing project knowledge: has_function/find_obj can distinguish real function symbols from translation-unit paths, avoiding a brittle slash-based heuristic.
Preserved current function-name workflow by keeping a single positional argument and only falling back to translation-unit mode when no matching function object is found.
Avoided overextending the feature by explicitly rejecting --write for translation-unit input rather than silently writing to the wrong source file.
Built translation-unit assembly paths with Path(m2c_input).with_suffix('.s'), matching the existing object-to-assembly convention used for functions.
Used m2c_args as an optional list so --function is only emitted when m2c should restrict output to a single function.
decomp_lessons: For decomp tooling that accepts either symbols or paths, prefer symbol-table/object-file checks over syntax heuristics when the repository already has reliable helpers such as has_function and find_obj.
Maintaining backward-compatible CLI behavior matters for frequently used scripts; adding translation-unit support did not require users to start passing --function for the existing common case.
Translation-unit decompilation should not automatically reuse function-level writeback logic. Source insertion for whole TUs needs separate design and should be disabled until implemented safely.
When adapting m2c calls, omit --function for whole translation units and include it only for function-scoped decompilation.
Error messages should shift from symbol-centric to path-centric when a tool starts accepting file-like inputs.
naming_conventions: The generalized positional CLI value was named m2c_input rather than function, reflecting that it may be a function symbol or translation-unit path.
The mode flag was named is_function, making downstream writeback gating explicit.
Existing project root constants such as OBJ_ROOT, ASM_ROOT, SRC_ROOT, and CTX_FILE remained the organizing convention for path construction.
Example function names follow project symbol style such as it_8026B9A8.
Example translation-unit inputs use source-relative paths without extension, such as melee/it/items/itheiho.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Function input path: find_obj scans object files and has_function identifies whether the requested symbol exists; the located object path is converted to the matching .s assembly file.
Translation-unit input path: if find_obj returns None, interpret the input as a relative assembly path under ASM_ROOT and append/replace with .s via with_suffix('.s').
m2c invocation tactic: pass '--function <name>' only when the input was confirmed to be a function; for TU input, pass the assembly file without --function.
Existence of the computed asm_file gates execution; missing TU paths now fail with an ASM-file path error instead of a missing-function error.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether --function could remain the default behavior because that is how the script currently works, and suggested the script could infer file paths since function names cannot contain slashes.
review_feedback.outcome: The final diff kept a single positional input and preserved function-first behavior. The author noted a better inference method: rely on has_function/find_obj rather than slash detection.

review_feedback.author: thefoxcam
review_feedback.feedback: Explained concern that slash detection could prevent root-level translation-unit input, then recognized that has_function and find_obj allow unambiguous classification.
review_feedback.outcome: Implemented inference by trying find_obj first and treating None as translation-unit input.

follow_up_queries: How should --write be implemented safely for translation-unit input in tools/decomp.py?
Are there root-level translation units where slash-based path inference would fail, validating the find_obj-first approach?
Should the type cast for args.m2c_input be str rather than bool in tools/decomp.py?
Should decomp.py validate translation-unit paths against SRC_ROOT or known build metadata before invoking m2c?
Can the error message distinguish between missing function symbols and missing translation-unit assembly files more explicitly?
